fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Kansas CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Kansas - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity kansas, how to transfer CNA license to kansas, kansas CNA endorsement application

board.name: Kansas KDADS
board.mailing_address:
Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Kansas.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Training Program
steps.description: Complete a 90-hour state-approved Nurse Aide training program in Kansas.

steps.title: Create a Pearson VUE Account
steps.description: Register for a web account on the Pearson VUE - Kansas Nurse Aide online portal.

steps.title: Submit Application and Schedule Exam
steps.description: Log in to your Pearson VUE account, complete the Application to Take the Nurse Aide Competency Examination, and schedule both the written/oral and clinical skills portions of the exam through the portal.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass both the written (or oral) and clinical skills components of the Kansas Nurse Aide Competency Examination.

steps.title: Registry Listing
steps.description: Upon successful completion of the examination, Pearson VUE will notify KDADS, and your certification will be added to the Kansas Nurse Aide Registry within one week.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Kansas CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $20. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Kansas.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Kansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kansas while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in KS.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Kansas.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Kansas
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant in Kansas, applicants must apply and schedule their certification exam through the Pearson VUE - Kansas Nurse Aide online portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Kansas
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Kansas CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Create a web account on the Pearson VUE - Kansas Nurse Aide portal, then log in to submit your application and schedule your exam.
